explanation: With a forecasted temperature of 28.9°C and news of a heatwave, demand is expected to be high. Current stock of 12 units is insufficient against the forecast of 36 units, necessitating a reorder to meet demand and maintain a safety buffer.
The forecasted temperature is 22.1°C with a baseline forecast of 22 units. Available stock of 36 units comfortably covers demand, even with heatwave news, ensuring a sufficient safety buffer.

With a forecasted temperature of 28.9°C and heatwave warnings, demand is likely to increase. Current stock of 18 units is inadequate for the forecast of 33 units, requiring a reorder to maintain stock levels and safety buffer.
Despite a high temperature of 35°C, available stock of 20 units covers the forecasted demand of 25 units. The safety buffer is adequate, and no reorder is needed.
With a forecasted temperature of 23.9°C and a baseline forecast of 18 units, the available stock of 27 units is sufficient to meet demand, maintaining a good safety buffer.

Forecasted temperature of 28.2°C and heatwave news suggest high demand. Current stock of 9 units is far below the forecast of 39 units, necessitating a significant reorder to meet demand and maintain a safety buffer.
